ucs.title: [LLM] Gamaredon (Aqua Blizzard / Shuckworm) Office remote template injection chain → mshta/wscript download cradle
ucs.description: Hunts Gamaredon's signature initial-access chain: spearphishing DOCX/RTF (often Ukrainian-language lures) abusing remote template injection (T1221) where WINWORD.EXE fetches a .dot/.dotm/.rtf template from attacker infrastructure on dynamic DNS or Russian TLDs, then spawns mshta.exe / wscript.exe / powershell.exe to stage Pterodo, GammaLoad, GammaDrop, or PteroPSDoor. The combination of an Office product making outbound HTTP to a *.ru / *.ddns.net / *.hopto.org / *.serveftp.com / *.online host AND immediately spawning a script interpreter is highly characteristic of this FSB Center 18 / SBU-at
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") by host All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.url All_Traffic.dest_port | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rex field=url "(?i)(?<sus>\.(ru|online|fun|space|site|website|ddns\.net|hopto\.org|serveftp\.com|myftp\.biz|sytes\.net))" | where isnotnull(sus) OR match(url,"(?i)\.(dot|dotm|rtf|docx|hta)(\?|$)") ] | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name process url dest | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let officeProcs = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let childProcs = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let susTlds = dynamic([".ru/",".online/",".fun/",".space/",".site/",".website/",".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".serveftp.com",".myftp.biz",".sytes.net"]);
let ProcSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (officeProcs)
| where FileName has_any (childProcs)
| project ProcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine;
let OfficeNet = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (officeProcs)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (susTlds) or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(dot|dotm|rtf|docx|hta)(\?|$)"
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName;
ProcSpawn
| join kind=inner OfficeNet on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', ProcTime, NetTime)) <= 180
| project ProcTime, NetTime, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Gamaredon is one of the few sustained operators that still relies on Office remote-template injection at scale (CERT-UA, Symantec/Shuckworm, Microsoft Aqua Blizzard reports). They consistently host stage-1 templates on dynamic-DNS providers (ddns.net, hopto.org, serveftp.com) and a long-running stable of Russian ccTLD domains, and detonate via mshta/wscript to drop the Pterodo/GammaLoad/GammaSteel

ucs.title: [LLM] Gamaredon USB worm (PteroLNK / GammaSteel) — LNK creation on removable media + wscript-launched VBS
ucs.description: Hunts Gamaredon's USB self-propagation module (Replication Through Removable Media, T1091), publicly tracked as PteroLNK / GammaSteel. The actor drops .lnk shortcuts on every connected removable drive that point to wscript.exe executing a hidden .vbs/.vbe payload, then beacons to fast-flux Telegram/Telegraph dead-drop resolvers and *.ru subdomains. We correlate LNK creation on a removable volume with subsequent script-interpreter execution from that same volume.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" AND Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified") AND (Filesystem.file_path="D:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="E:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="F:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="G:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="H:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="I:\\*") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe") AND (Processes.process="*.vbs*" OR Processes.process="*.vbe*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*.lnk*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe") (All_Traffic.url="*telegra.ph*" OR All_Traffic.url="*t.me*" OR All_Traffic.url="*telegram.me*" OR All_Traffic.url="*.ru/*" OR All_Traffic.url="*.online/*" OR All_Traffic.url="*ddns.net*") by host All_Traffic.url All_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` ] | stats values(file_path) as lnk_paths values(process) as scripts values(url) as urls min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by host user | where isnotnull(lnk_paths) AND isnotnull(scripts)
ucs.kql:
let LnkOnRemovable = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FileName endswith ".lnk"
| where FolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Z]:\\"
| project LnkTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, LnkPath=FolderPath, LnkName=FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let ScriptExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".hta",".js",".lnk")
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"^[D-Z]:\\"
| project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
let DeadDropC2 = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe")
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("telegra.ph","t.me","telegram.me")
   or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)://[a-z0-9-]+\.(ru|online|fun|space|site|website)(/|:)"
   or RemoteUrl has_any (".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".serveftp.com")
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP;
LnkOnRemovable
| join kind=inner ScriptExec on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('minute', ExecTime, LnkTime) between (-30 .. 30)
| join kind=leftouter DeadDropC2 on DeviceId
| project LnkTime, ExecTime, NetTime, DeviceName, AccountName, LnkPath, LnkName, ChildProc=FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by LnkTime desc
ucs.rationale: USB-borne LNK propagation tied to Telegram/Telegraph dead-drop resolvers is a near-fingerprint of Gamaredon. Symantec (Shuckworm), Cisco Talos, ESET, and Palo Alto Unit 42 (Trident Ursa) have repeatedly documented PteroLNK and GammaSteel using exactly this pattern: a worm component that enumerates removable drives, plants .lnk shortcuts that call wscript.exe with a hidden .vbs, and resolves rotati
